    14→      </div>
    15→
    16→      <!-- Right: Document type + template switchers + action slot -->
    17→      <div class="flex items-center gap-3">
    18→        <!-- Document Type Dropdown -->

    20→          <label class="text-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-gray-500 whitespace-nowrap">Type</label>
    21→          <USelectMenu
    22→            v-model="selectedDocumentType"
    23→            :items="documentTypeOptions"
    24→            value-key="value"
    25→            label-key="label"
    26→            size="xs"
    27→            class="w-36"
    28→          />
    29→        </div>
    30→
    31→        <!-- Template Switcher Dropdown (filtered by active document type) -->
    32→        <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
    33→          <label class="text-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-gray-500 whitespace-nowrap">Template</label>
    34→          <USelectMenu
    35→            v-model="selectedTemplate"
    36→            :items="templateOptions"
    37→            value-key="value"
    38→            label-key="label"
    39→            size="xs"
    40→            class="w-28"
    41→          />
    42→        </div>
    43→
    44→        <slot name="right-actions" />
    45→      </div>
    46→    </div>
    47→  </UContainer>
    48→</template>
    49→
    50→<style scoped>
    51→.app-header {
    52→  padding-top: 0.5rem;
    53→  padding-bottom: 0.5rem;
    54→}
    55→</style>
    56→
    57→<script setup lang="ts">
    58→import { computed } from 'vue'
    59→import { useTemplate } from '~/composables/useTemplate'
    60→import { useDocumentType } from '~/composables/useDocumentType'
    61→
    62→const { activeTemplate, setActiveTemplate, getTemplatesForDocumentType } = useTemplate()
    63→const { activeDocumentType, setActiveDocumentType, getAvailableDocumentTypes } = useDocumentType()
    64→
    65→// Document type options (static — registry doesn't change at runtime)
    66→const documentTypeOptions = getAvailableDocumentTypes().map(d => ({ label: d.displayName, value: d.name }))
    67→
    68→// Template options re-computed whenever the active document type changes
    69→const templateOptions = computed(() =>
    70→  getTemplatesForDocumentType(activeDocumentType.value).map(m => ({ label: m.displayName, value: m.name })),
    71→)
    72→
    73→const selectedDocumentType = computed({
    74→  get: () => activeDocumentType.value,
    75→  set: (val: string) => setActiveDocumentType(val),
    76→})
    77→
    78→const selectedTemplate = computed({
    79→  get: () => activeTemplate.value,
    80→  set: (val: string) => setActiveTemplate(val),
    81→})
    82→</script>
    83→

USERMESSAGE #9
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Template System Composable
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides reactive template selection and management for document rendering.
     5→ * Active template is shared across all components via useState and persisted
     6→ * to localStorage so the selection survives page reloads.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import { onMounted, computed } from 'vue'
    10→import type { Component } from 'vue'
    11→import CoverLetterModern from '~/templates/modern/CoverLetterModern.vue'
    12→import CoverLetterClassic from '~/templates/classic/CoverLetterClassic.vue'
    13→import CoverLetterMinimal from '~/templates/minimal/CoverLetterMinimal.vue'
    14→import CoverLetterExecutive from '~/templates/executive/CoverLetterExecutive.vue'
    15→import CoverLetterCreative from '~/templates/creative/CoverLetterCreative.vue'
    16→import ResignationProfessional from '~/templates/resignation-professional/ResignationProfessional.vue'
    17→import ResignationBrief from '~/templates/resignation-brief/ResignationBrief.vue'
    18→
    19→const LS_KEY = 'ohmydoc_template'
    20→
    21→// Template metadata interface
    22→export interface TemplateMetadata {
    23→  name: string
    24→  displayName: string
    25→  description: string
    26→  /** Document type names this template supports (matches DocumentTypeConfig.name) */
    27→  documentTypes: string[]
    28→}
    29→
    30→// Template registry type
    31→export interface TemplateRegistry {
    32→  [key: string]: {
    33→    component: Component
    34→    metadata: TemplateMetadata
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Template Registry
    40→ *
    41→ * Maps template names to their Vue components and metadata.
    42→ * All templates use the same ParsedData props interface for consistency.
    43→ */
    44→const templates: TemplateRegistry = {
    45→  modern: {
    46→    component: CoverLetterModern,
    47→    metadata: {
    48→      name: 'modern',
    49→      displayName: 'Modern',
    50→      description: 'Professional cover letter template with modern styling and clean typography',
    51→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    52→    },
    53→  },
    54→  classic: {
    55→    component: CoverLetterClassic,
    56→    metadata: {
    57→      name: 'classic',
    58→      displayName: 'Classic',
    59→      description: 'Traditional cover letter with table-based layout and formal styling',
    60→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    61→    },
    62→  },
    63→  minimal: {
    64→    component: CoverLetterMinimal,
    65→    metadata: {
    66→      name: 'minimal',
    67→      displayName: 'Minimal',
    68→      description: 'Clean, minimalist cover letter design with simple structure and generous whitespace',
    69→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    70→    },
    71→  },
    72→  executive: {
    73→    component: CoverLetterExecutive,
    74→    metadata: {
    75→      name: 'executive',
    76→      displayName: 'Executive',
    77→      description: 'Bold, confident serif template with navy accent, large name treatment, and horizontal rule dividers',
    78→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    79→    },
    80→  },
    81→  creative: {
    82→    component: CoverLetterCreative,
    83→    metadata: {
    84→      name: 'creative',
    85→      displayName: 'Creative',
    86→      description: 'Two-column layout with vibrant accent sidebar — designed for designers, creatives, and marketers',
    87→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    88→    },
    89→  },
    90→  'resignation-professional': {
    91→    component: ResignationProfessional,
    92→    metadata: {
    93→      name: 'resignation-professional',
    94→      displayName: 'Professional',
    95→      description: 'Formal letterhead layout with sender top-left, date top-right, and clean typography',
    96→      documentTypes: ['resignation-letter'],
    97→    },
    98→  },
    99→  'resignation-brief': {
   100→    component: ResignationBrief,
   101→    metadata: {
   102→      name: 'resignation-brief',
   103→      displayName: 'Brief',
   104→      description: 'Compact, minimal resignation letter with just the essentials',
   105→      documentTypes: ['resignation-letter'],
   106→    },
   107→  },
   108→}
   109→
   110→/**
   111→ * Composable function to use the reactive template system in Vue components.
   112→ *
   113→ * Active template state is shared across all component instances via useState.
   114→ * On mount, the saved localStorage value is restored (client-side only).
   115→ *
   116→ * @returns Reactive template state and control functions
   117→ */
   118→export function useTemplate() {
   119→  // Shared reactive state — useState ensures all components see the same value
   120→  const activeTemplate = useState<string>('activeTemplate', () => 'modern')
   121→
   122→  // Restore from localStorage after hydration (client-only, runs once per component mount)
   123→  onMounted(() => {
   124→    try {
   125→      const saved = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY)
   126→      if (saved && templates[saved]) {
   127→        activeTemplate.value = saved
   128→      }
   129→    }
   130→    catch {
   131→      // Ignore storage errors (private browsing, quota exceeded, etc.)
   132→    }
   133→  })
   134→
   135→  /**
   136→   * Switch to a different template and persist the choice to localStorage.
   137→   */
   138→  function setActiveTemplate(name: string) {
   139→    if (!templates[name]) {
   140→      console.warn(`Template "${name}" not found. Available: ${Object.keys(templates).join(', ')}`)
   141→      return
   142→    }
   143→    activeTemplate.value = name
   144→    try {
   145→      localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, name)
   146→    }
   147→    catch {
   148→      // Ignore storage errors
   149→    }
   150→  }
   151→
   152→  /** Reactive component for the currently active template */
   153→  const currentTemplate = computed<Component | undefined>(
   154→    () => templates[activeTemplate.value]?.component,
   155→  )
   156→
   157→  /** Metadata for the currently active template */
   158→  const currentTemplateMetadata = computed<TemplateMetadata | undefined>(
   159→    () => templates[activeTemplate.value]?.metadata,
   160→  )
   161→
   162→  /** Names of all registered templates */
   163→  function getAvailableTemplates(): string[] {
   164→    return Object.keys(templates)
   165→  }
   166→
   167→  /** Metadata objects for all registered templates */
   168→  function getAllTemplateMetadata(): TemplateMetadata[] {
   169→    return Object.values(templates).map(t => t.metadata)
   170→  }
   171→
   172→  /** Metadata objects for templates that support the given document type */
   173→  function getTemplatesForDocumentType(docType: string): TemplateMetadata[] {
   174→    return Object.values(templates)
   175→      .filter(t => t.metadata.documentTypes.includes(docType))
   176→      .map(t => t.metadata)
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    activeTemplate,
   181→    setActiveTemplate,
   182→    currentTemplate,
   183→    currentTemplateMetadata,
   184→    getAvailableTemplates,
   185→    getAllTemplateMetadata,
   186→    getTemplatesForDocumentType,
   187→  }
   188→}
   189→

   30\u2192// Template registry type\n    31\u2192export interface TemplateRegistry {\n    32\u2192  [key: string]: {\n    33\u2192    component: Component\n    34\u2192    metadata: TemplateMetadata\n    35\u2192  }\n    36\u2192}\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192/**\n    39\u2192 * Template Registry\n    40\u2192 *\n    41\u2192 * Maps template names to their Vue components and metadata.\n    42\u2192 * All templates use the same ParsedData props interface for consistency.\n    43\u2192 */\n    44\u2192const templates: TemplateRegistry = {\n    45\u2192  modern: {\n    46\u2192    component: CoverLetterModern,\n    47\u2192    metadata: {\n    48\u2192      name: 'modern',\n    49\u2192      displayName: 'Modern',\n    50\u2192      description: 'Professional cover letter template with modern styling and clean typography',\n    51\u2192      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],\n    52\u2192    },\n    53\u2192  },\n    54\u2192  classic: {\n    55\u2192    component: CoverLetterClassic,\n    56\u2192    metadata: {\n    57\u2192      name: 'classic',\n    58\u2192      displayName: 'Classic',\n    59\u2192      description: 'Traditional cover letter with table-based layout and formal styling',\n    60\u2192      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],\n    61\u2192    },\n    62\u2192  },\n    63\u2192  minimal: {\n    64\u2192    component: CoverLetterMinimal,\n    65\u2192    metadata: {\n    66\u2192      name: 'minimal',\n    67\u2192      displayName: 'Minimal',\n    68\u2192      description: 'Clean, minimalist cover letter design with simple structure and generous whitespace',\n    69\u2192      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],\n    70\u2192    },\n    71\u2192  },\n    72\u2192  executive: {\n    73\u2192    component: CoverLetterExecutive,\n    74\u2192    metadata: {\n    75\u2192      name: 'executive',\n    76\u2192      displayName: 'Executive',\n    77\u2192      description: 'Bold, confident serif template with navy accent, large name treatment, and horizontal rule dividers',\n    78\u2192      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],\n    79\u2192    },\n    80\u2192  },\n    81\u2192  creative: {\n    82\u2192    component: CoverLetterCreative,\n    83\u2192    metadata: {\n    84\u2192      name: 'creative',\n    85\u2192      displayName: 'Creative',\n    86\u2192      description: 'Two-column layout with vibrant accent sidebar \u2014 designed for designers, creatives, and marketers',\n    87\u2192      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],\n    88\u2192    },\n    89\u2192  },\n    90\u2192  'resignation-professional': {\n    91\u2192    component: ResignationProfessional,\n    92\u2192    metadata: {\n    93\u2192      name: 'resignation-professional',\n    94\u2192      displayName: 'Professional',\n    95\u2192      description: 'Formal letterhead layout with sender top-left, date top-right, and clean typography',\n    96\u2192      documentTypes: ['resignation-letter'],\n    97\u2192    },\n    98\u2192  },\n    99\u2192  'resignation-brief': {\n   100\u2192    component: ResignationBrief,\n   101\u2192    metadata: {\n   102\u2192      name: 'resignation-brief',\n   103\u2192      displayName: 'Brief',\n   104\u2192      description: 'Compact, minimal resignation letter with just the essentials',\n   105\u2192      documentTypes: ['resignation-letter'],\n   106\u2192    },\n   107\u2192  },\n   108\u2192}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192/**\n   111\u2192 * Composable function to use the reactive template system in Vue components.\n   112\u2192 *\n   113\u2192 * Active template state is shared across all component instances via useState.\n   114\u2192 * On mount, the saved localStorage value is restored (client-side only).\n   115\u2192 *\n   116\u2192 * @returns Reactive template state and control functions\n   117\u2192 */\n   118\u2192export function useTemplate() {\n   119\u2192  // Shared reactive state \u2014 useState ensures all components see the same value\n   120\u2192  const activeTemplate = useState<string>('activeTemplate', () => 'modern')\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192  // Restore from localStorage after hydration (client-only, runs once per component mount)\n   123\u2192  onMounted(() => {\n   124\u2192    try {\n   125\u2192      const saved = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY)\n   126\u2192      if (saved && templates[saved]) {\n   127\u2192        activeTemplate.value = saved\n   128\u2192      }\n   129\u2192    }\n   130\u2192    catch {\n   131\u2192      // Ignore storage errors (private browsing, quota exceeded, etc.)\n   132\u2192    }\n   133\u2192  })\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  /**\n   136\u2192   * Switch to a different template and persist the choice to localStorage.\n   137\u2192   */\n   138\u2192  function setActiveTemplate(name: string) {\n   139\u2192    if (!templates[name]) {\n   140\u2192      console.warn(`Template \"${name}\" not found. Available: ${Object.keys(templates).join(', ')}`)\n   141\u2192      return\n   142\u2192    }\n   143\u2192    activeTemplate.value = name\n   144\u2192    try {\n   145\u2192      localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, name)\n   146\u2192    }\n   147\u2192    catch {\n   148\u2192      // Ignore storage errors\n   149\u2192    }\n   150\u2192  }\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192  /** Reactive component for the currently active template */\n   153\u2192  const currentTemplate = computed<Component | undefined>(\n   154\u2192    () => templates[activeTemplate.value]?.component,\n   155\u2192  )\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192  /** Metadata for the currently active template */\n   158\u2192  const currentTemplateMetadata = computed<TemplateMetadata | undefined>(\n   159\u2192    () => templates[activeTemplate.value]?.metadata,\n   160\u2192  )\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192  /** Names of all registered templates */\n   163\u2192  function getAvailableTemplates(): string[] {\n   164\u2192    return Object.keys(templates)\n   165\u2192  }\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192  /** Metadata objects for all registered templates */\n   168\u2192  function getAllTemplateMetadata(): TemplateMetadata[] {\n   169\u2192    return Object.values(templates).map(t => t.metadata)\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  /** Metadata objects for templates that support the given document type */\n   173\u2192  function getTemplatesForDocumentType(docType: string): TemplateMetadata[] {\n   174\u2192    return Object.values(templates)\n   175\u2192      .filter(t => t.metadata.documentTypes.includes(docType))\n   176\u2192      .map(t => t.metadata)\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  return {\n   180\u2192    activeTemplate,\n   181\u2192    setActiveTemplate,\n   182\u2192    currentTemplate,\n   183\u2192    currentTemplateMetadata,\n   184\u2192    getAvailableTemplates,\n   185\u2192    getAllTemplateMetadata,\n   186\u2192    getTemplatesForDocumentType,\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192}\n   189\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

SUCCESS 
     1→<template>
     2→  <div class="app-container">
     3→    <!-- AppHeader with title, tagline, and action slots -->

     7→        <!-- Empty slot - will be populated in MVP 8 -->
     8→      </template>
     9→
    10→      <!-- Right action slot: Export PDF button -->

    12→        <UButton
    13→          size="xs"
    14→          variant="outline"
    15→          icon="i-heroicons-printer"
    16→          @click="handlePrint"
    17→        >
    18→          Export PDF
    19→        </UButton>
    20→      </template>
    21→    </AppHeader>
    22→
    23→    <!-- Welcome Screen: shown on first load when no saved data exists -->
    24→    <div v-if="showWelcome" class="welcome-screen">
    25→      <div class="welcome-card">
    26→        <div class="welcome-icon">
    27→          <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="48" height="48" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
    28→            <path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z" />
    29→            <polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8" />
    30→            <line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13" />
    31→            <line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17" />
    32→            <polyline points="10 9 9 9 8 9" />
    33→          </svg>
    34→        </div>
    35→        <h2 class="welcome-headline">
    36→          Create professional documents in seconds
    37→        </h2>
    38→        <p class="welcome-description">
    39→          Write structured XML, pick a template, and instantly preview a polished document — ready to export.
    40→        </p>
    41→        <UButton
    42→          size="xl"
    43→          color="primary"
    44→          class="welcome-cta"
    45→          :loading="isSampleLoading"
    46→          @click="loadSample"
    47→        >
    48→          Start with a sample
    49→        </UButton>
    50→      </div>
    51→    </div>
    52→
    53→    <!-- Dual-panel layout: Editor (left) and Preview (right) -->
    54→    <div v-else class="dual-panel-layout">
    55→      <!-- Left Panel: XML Editor -->
    56→      <div class="editor-panel">
    57→        <XmlEditor
    58→          v-model="xmlContent"
    59→          height="100%"
    60→          width="100%"
    61→        />
    62→      </div>
    63→
    64→      <!-- Right Panel: Preview -->
    65→      <div class="preview-panel">
    66→        <PreviewPanel
    67→          :xml-content="debouncedXmlContent"
    68→          :zoom="1"
    69→        />
    70→      </div>
    71→    </div>
    72→  </div>
    73→</template>
    74→
    75→<script setup lang="ts">
    76→import { ref, watch, onMounted, onUnmounted } from 'vue'
    77→import { useDocumentType } from '~/composables/useDocumentType'
    78→
    79→/**
    80→ * Main Application Page - MVP 6: Dual-Panel Integration
    81→ *
    82→ * Features:
    83→ * - Welcome screen on first load (no saved data in localStorage)
    84→ * - Dual-panel 50/50 layout (CSS Grid)
    85→ * - 300ms debounced real-time updates (per DECISIONS.md)
    86→ * - Persists XML content to localStorage
    87→ * - Minimum 1024px responsive design
    88→ * - Document type switching loads the appropriate sample XML
    89→ */
    90→
    91→const LS_KEY = 'ohmydoc_xml_content'
    92→
    93→function handlePrint() {
    94→  window.print()
    95→}
    96→
    97→const { activeDocumentType, currentDocumentType } = useDocumentType()
    98→
    99→// Set page metadata
   100→useHead({
   101→  title: 'OhMyDoc - XML to HTML Transformer',
   102→})
   103→
   104→// Welcome screen is shown when no saved data exists
   105→const showWelcome = ref(true)
   106→const isSampleLoading = ref(false)
   107→
   108→// Reactive state for XML content
   109→const xmlContent = ref('')
   110→const debouncedXmlContent = ref('')
   111→
   112→// Debounce timer ref
   113→let debounceTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null
   114→
   115→/**
   116→ * Debounce watcher: Updates preview 300ms after user stops typing
   117→ * Per DECISIONS.md Decision 5: 300ms < 500ms threshold provides smooth UX
   118→ * without feeling laggy or causing excessive re-parsing
   119→ */
   120→watch(xmlContent, (newValue) => {
   121→  if (debounceTimer) {
   122→    clearTimeout(debounceTimer)
   123→  }
   124→
   125→  debounceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   126→    debouncedXmlContent.value = newValue
   127→    // Persist to localStorage so returning users skip the welcome screen
   128→    try {
   129→      localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, newValue)
   130→    }
   131→    catch {
   132→      // Ignore storage errors (private browsing, quota exceeded, etc.)
   133→    }
   134→  }, 300)
   135→})
   136→
   137→/**
   138→ * Fetch and apply the sample XML for the active document type.
   139→ * Called by the "Start with a sample" button and when switching document types.
   140→ */
   141→async function loadSample() {
   142→  isSampleLoading.value = true
   143→  const samplePath = currentDocumentType.value?.sampleXmlPath ?? '/samples/cover-letter.xml'
   144→  try {
   145→    const response = await fetch(samplePath)
   146→    if (!response.ok) {
   147→      throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`)
   148→    }
   149→    const sampleXml = await response.text()
   150→    xmlContent.value = sampleXml
   151→    debouncedXmlContent.value = sampleXml
   152→    showWelcome.value = false
   153→  }
   154→  catch (error) {
   155→    console.error('Error loading sample XML:', error)
   156→    // Still dismiss welcome and show an empty editor rather than getting stuck
   157→    showWelcome.value = false
   158→  }
   159→  finally {
   160→    isSampleLoading.value = false
   161→  }
   162→}
   163→
   164→/**
   165→ * When the user switches document type from the header dropdown, load the
   166→ * sample XML for the newly selected type so there's always a valid document
   167→ * to preview. Only fires on explicit user action (not on initial page load).
   168→ */
   169→watch(activeDocumentType, () => {
   170→  if (!showWelcome.value) {
   171→    loadSample()
   172→  }
   173→}, { immediate: false })
   174→
   175→/**
   176→ * On mount: restore saved content from localStorage.
   177→ * If content exists, skip the welcome screen and load it directly.
   178→ */
   179→onMounted(() => {
   180→  try {
   181→    const saved = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY)
   182→    if (saved) {
   183→      xmlContent.value = saved
   184→      debouncedXmlContent.value = saved
   185→      showWelcome.value = false
   186→    }
   187→  }
   188→  catch {
   189→    // Ignore localStorage errors; welcome screen will show as default
   190→  }
   191→})
   192→
   193→/**
   194→ * Cleanup: Clear debounce timer on component unmount
   195→ */
   196→onUnmounted(() => {
   197→  if (debounceTimer) {
   198→    clearTimeout(debounceTimer)
   199→  }
   200→})
   201→</script>
   202→
   203→<style scoped>
   204→/**
   205→ * Main app container - full viewport height
   206→ */
   207→.app-container {
   208→  display: flex;
   209→  flex-direction: column;
   210→  height: 100vh;
   211→  width: 100vw;
   212→  overflow: hidden;
   213→}
   214→
   215→/**
   216→ * Dual-panel layout using CSS Grid
   217→ * 50/50 split between editor (left) and preview (right)
   218→ * Minimum width: 1024px total (512px per panel)
   219→ */
   220→.dual-panel-layout {
   221→  display: grid;
   222→  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
   223→  flex: 1;
   224→  min-height: 0;
   225→  gap: 0;
   226→
   227→  /* Minimum width constraint */
   228→  min-width: 1024px;
   229→}
   230→
   231→/**
   232→ * Left panel: XML Editor
   233→ * Full height, scrollable if needed
   234→ */
   235→.editor-panel {
   236→  display: flex;
   237→  flex-direction: column;
   238→  overflow: hidden;
   239→  background-color: #1e1e1e; /* Dark editor background */
   240→  border-right: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);
   241→  transition: border-color 0.15s ease;
   242→}
   243→
   244→/**
   245→ * Right panel: Preview
   246→ * Full height, scrollable if needed
   247→ */
   248→.preview-panel {
   249→  display: flex;
   250→  flex-direction: column;
   251→  overflow: auto;
   252→  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);
   253→  transition: background-color 0.15s ease;
   254→}
   255→
   256→/**
   257→ * Print: hide all editor chrome, show only the preview document
   258→ *
   259→ * - Hide header (dropdowns, buttons, title)
   260→ * - Hide the XML editor panel
   261→ * - Collapse the dual-panel grid to a single column
   262→ * - Let the preview fill the full printable area
   263→ * - @page margin (in app.vue) provides the physical page margins
   264→ */
   265→@media print {
   266→  .app-container {
   267→    display: block;
   268→    height: auto;
   269→    overflow: visible;
   270→  }
   271→
   272→  /* Hide entire app header (title, type/template dropdowns, export button) */
   273→  .app-header {
   274→    display: none;
   275→  }
   276→
   277→  /* Hide the XML editor */
   278→  .editor-panel {
   279→    display: none;
   280→  }
   281→
   282→  /* Collapse the two-column grid; let preview fill the page */
   283→  .dual-panel-layout {
   284→    display: block;
   285→    min-width: unset;
   286→  }
   287→
   288→  /* Strip the preview wrapper background so only the document shows */
   289→  .preview-panel {
   290→    background: transparent;
   291→  }
   292→
   293→  /* Hide the welcome screen if visible */
   294→  .welcome-screen {
   295→    display: none;
   296→  }
   297→}
   298→
   299→/**
   300→ * Responsive design for smaller screens
   301→ * Stack panels vertically below 1024px
   302→ */
   303→@media (max-width: 1024px) {
   304→  .dual-panel-layout {
   305→    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
   306→    grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;
   307→    min-width: unset;
   308→  }
   309→
   310→  .editor-panel {
   311→    border-right: none;
   312→    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-gray-300);
   313→  }
   314→}
   315→
   316→/**
   317→ * Welcome screen - shown on first load
   318→ */
   319→.welcome-screen {
   320→  flex: 1;
   321→  display: flex;
   322→  align-items: center;
   323→  justify-content: center;
   324→  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);
   325→  padding: 2rem;
   326→}
   327→
   328→.welcome-card {
   329→  display: flex;
   330→  flex-direction: column;
   331→  align-items: center;
   332→  text-align: center;
   333→  max-width: 480px;
   334→  gap: 1.25rem;
   335→}
   336→
   337→.welcome-icon {
   338→  color: var(--color-primary-500, #0f6fec);
   339→  opacity: 0.85;
   340→}
   341→
   342→.welcome-headline {
   343→  font-size: 1.75rem;
   344→  font-weight: 700;
   345→  line-height: 1.25;
   346→  color: var(--color-gray-900);
   347→  margin: 0;
   348→}
   349→
   350→.welcome-description {
   351→  font-size: 1rem;
   352→  line-height: 1.6;
   353→  color: var(--color-gray-600);
   354→  margin: 0;
   355→}
   356→
   357→.welcome-cta {
   358→  margin-top: 0.5rem;
   359→}
   360→</style>
   361→

         width=\"100%\"\n    61\u2192        />\n    62\u2192      </div>\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192      <!-- Right Panel: Preview -->\n    65\u2192      <div class=\"preview-panel\">\n    66\u2192        <PreviewPanel\n    67\u2192          :xml-content=\"debouncedXmlContent\"\n    68\u2192          :zoom=\"1\"\n    69\u2192        />\n    70\u2192      </div>\n    71\u2192    </div>\n    72\u2192  </div>\n    73\u2192</template>\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192<script setup lang=\"ts\">\n    76\u2192import { ref, watch, onMounted, onUnmounted } from 'vue'\n    77\u2192import { useDocumentType } from '~/composables/useDocumentType'\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192/**\n    80\u2192 * Main Application Page - MVP 6: Dual-Panel Integration\n    81\u2192 *\n    82\u2192 * Features:\n    83\u2192 * - Welcome screen on first load (no saved data in localStorage)\n    84\u2192 * - Dual-panel 50/50 layout (CSS Grid)\n    85\u2192 * - 300ms debounced real-time updates (per DECISIONS.md)\n    86\u2192 * - Persists XML content to localStorage\n    87\u2192 * - Minimum 1024px responsive design\n    88\u2192 * - Document type switching loads the appropriate sample XML\n    89\u2192 */\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192const LS_KEY = 'ohmydoc_xml_content'\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192function handlePrint() {\n    94\u2192  window.print()\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192const { activeDocumentType, currentDocumentType } = useDocumentType()\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// Set page metadata\n   100\u2192useHead({\n   101\u2192  title: 'OhMyDoc - XML to HTML Transformer',\n   102\u2192})\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192// Welcome screen is shown when no saved data exists\n   105\u2192const showWelcome = ref(true)\n   106\u2192const isSampleLoading = ref(false)\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192// Reactive state for XML content\n   109\u2192const xmlContent = ref('')\n   110\u2192const debouncedXmlContent = ref('')\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192// Debounce timer ref\n   113\u2192let debounceTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192/**\n   116\u2192 * Debounce watcher: Updates preview 300ms after user stops typing\n   117\u2192 * Per DECISIONS.md Decision 5: 300ms < 500ms threshold provides smooth UX\n   118\u2192 * without feeling laggy or causing excessive re-parsing\n   119\u2192 */\n   120\u2192watch(xmlContent, (newValue) => {\n   121\u2192  if (debounceTimer) {\n   122\u2192    clearTimeout(debounceTimer)\n   123\u2192  }\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  debounceTimer = setTimeout(() => {\n   126\u2192    debouncedXmlContent.value = newValue\n   127\u2192    // Persist to localStorage so returning users skip the welcome screen\n   128\u2192    try {\n   129\u2192      localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, newValue)\n   130\u2192    }\n   131\u2192    catch {\n   132\u2192      // Ignore storage errors (private browsing, quota exceeded, etc.)\n   133\u2192    }\n   134\u2192  }, 300)\n   135\u2192})\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192/**\n   138\u2192 * Fetch and apply the sample XML for the active document type.\n   139\u2192 * Called by the \"Start with a sample\" button and when switching document types.\n   140\u2192 */\n   141\u2192async function loadSample() {\n   142\u2192  isSampleLoading.value = true\n   143\u2192  const samplePath = currentDocumentType.value?.sampleXmlPath ?? '/samples/cover-letter.xml'\n   144\u2192  try {\n   145\u2192    const response = await fetch(samplePath)\n   146\u2192    if (!response.ok) {\n   147\u2192      throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`)\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192    const sampleXml = await response.text()\n   150\u2192    xmlContent.value = sampleXml\n   151\u2192    debouncedXmlContent.value = sampleXml\n   152\u2192    showWelcome.value = false\n   153\u2192  }\n   154\u2192  catch (error) {\n   155\u2192    console.error('Error loading sample XML:', error)\n   156\u2192    // Still dismiss welcome and show an empty editor rather than getting stuck\n   157\u2192    showWelcome.value = false\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192  finally {\n   160\u2192    isSampleLoading.value = false\n   161\u2192  }\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192/**\n   165\u2192 * When the user switches document type from the header dropdown, load the\n   166\u2192 * sample XML for the newly selected type so there's always a valid document\n   167\u2192 * to preview. Only fires on explicit user action (not on initial page load).\n   168\u2192 */\n   169\u2192watch(activeDocumentType, () => {\n   170\u2192  if (!showWelcome.value) {\n   171\u2192    loadSample()\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192}, { immediate: false })\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192/**\n   176\u2192 * On mount: restore saved content from localStorage.\n   177\u2192 * If content exists, skip the welcome screen and load it directly.\n   178\u2192 */\n   179\u2192onMounted(() => {\n   180\u2192  try {\n   181\u2192    const saved = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY)\n   182\u2192    if (saved) {\n   183\u2192      xmlContent.value = saved\n   184\u2192      debouncedXmlContent.value = saved\n   185\u2192      showWelcome.value = false\n   186\u2192    }\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192  catch {\n   189\u2192    // Ignore localStorage errors; welcome screen will show as default\n   190\u2192  }\n   191\u2192})\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192/**\n   194\u2192 * Cleanup: Clear debounce timer on component unmount\n   195\u2192 */\n   196\u2192onUnmounted(() => {\n   197\u2192  if (debounceTimer) {\n   198\u2192    clearTimeout(debounceTimer)\n   199\u2192  }\n   200\u2192})\n   201\u2192</script>\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192<style scoped>\n   204\u2192/**\n   205\u2192 * Main app container - full viewport height\n   206\u2192 */\n   207\u2192.app-container {\n   208\u2192  display: flex;\n   209\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   210\u2192  height: 100vh;\n   211\u2192  width: 100vw;\n   212\u2192  overflow: hidden;\n   213\u2192}\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192/**\n   216\u2192 * Dual-panel layout using CSS Grid\n   217\u2192 * 50/50 split between editor (left) and preview (right)\n   218\u2192 * Minimum width: 1024px total (512px per panel)\n   219\u2192 */\n   220\u2192.dual-panel-layout {\n   221\u2192  display: grid;\n   222\u2192  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n   223\u2192  flex: 1;\n   224\u2192  min-height: 0;\n   225\u2192  gap: 0;\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192  /* Minimum width constraint */\n   228\u2192  min-width: 1024px;\n   229\u2192}\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192/**\n   232\u2192 * Left panel: XML Editor\n   233\u2192 * Full height, scrollable if needed\n   234\u2192 */\n   235\u2192.editor-panel {\n   236\u2192  display: flex;\n   237\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   238\u2192  overflow: hidden;\n   239\u2192  background-color: #1e1e1e; /* Dark editor background */\n   240\u2192  border-right: 1px solid var(--color-gray-200);\n   241\u2192  transition: border-color 0.15s ease;\n   242\u2192}\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192/**\n   245\u2192 * Right panel: Preview\n   246\u2192 * Full height, scrollable if needed\n   247\u2192 */\n   248\u2192.preview-panel {\n   249\u2192  display: flex;\n   250\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   251\u2192  overflow: auto;\n   252\u2192  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);\n   253\u2192  transition: background-color 0.15s ease;\n   254\u2192}\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192/**\n   257\u2192 * Print: hide all editor chrome, show only the preview document\n   258\u2192 *\n   259\u2192 * - Hide header (dropdowns, buttons, title)\n   260\u2192 * - Hide the XML editor panel\n   261\u2192 * - Collapse the dual-panel grid to a single column\n   262\u2192 * - Let the preview fill the full printable area\n   263\u2192 * - @page margin (in app.vue) provides the physical page margins\n   264\u2192 */\n   265\u2192@media print {\n   266\u2192  .app-container {\n   267\u2192    display: block;\n   268\u2192    height: auto;\n   269\u2192    overflow: visible;\n   270\u2192  }\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192  /* Hide entire app header (title, type/template dropdowns, export button) */\n   273\u2192  .app-header {\n   274\u2192    display: none;\n   275\u2192  }\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  /* Hide the XML editor */\n   278\u2192  .editor-panel {\n   279\u2192    display: none;\n   280\u2192  }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  /* Collapse the two-column grid; let preview fill the page */\n   283\u2192  .dual-panel-layout {\n   284\u2192    display: block;\n   285\u2192    min-width: unset;\n   286\u2192  }\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192  /* Strip the preview wrapper background so only the document shows */\n   289\u2192  .preview-panel {\n   290\u2192    background: transparent;\n   291\u2192  }\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192  /* Hide the welcome screen if visible */\n   294\u2192  .welcome-screen {\n   295\u2192    display: none;\n   296\u2192  }\n   297\u2192}\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192/**\n   300\u2192 * Responsive design for smaller screens\n   301\u2192 * Stack panels vertically below 1024px\n   302\u2192 */\n   303\u2192@media (max-width: 1024px) {\n   304\u2192  .dual-panel-layout {\n   305\u2192    grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n   306\u2192    grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;\n   307\u2192    min-width: unset;\n   308\u2192  }\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192  .editor-panel {\n   311\u2192    border-right: none;\n   312\u2192    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-gray-300);\n   313\u2192  }\n   314\u2192}\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192/**\n   317\u2192 * Welcome screen - shown on first load\n   318\u2192 */\n   319\u2192.welcome-screen {\n   320\u2192  flex: 1;\n   321\u2192  display: flex;\n   322\u2192  align-items: center;\n   323\u2192  justify-content: center;\n   324\u2192  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);\n   325\u2192  padding: 2rem;\n   326\u2192}\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192.welcome-card {\n   329\u2192  display: flex;\n   330\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   331\u2192  align-items: center;\n   332\u2192  text-align: center;\n   333\u2192  max-width: 480px;\n   334\u2192  gap: 1.25rem;\n   335\u2192}\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192.welcome-icon {\n   338\u2192  color: var(--color-primary-500, #0f6fec);\n   339\u2192  opacity: 0.85;\n   340\u2192}\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192.welcome-headline {\n   343\u2192  font-size: 1.75rem;\n   344\u2192  font-weight: 700;\n   345\u2192  line-height: 1.25;\n   346\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-900);\n   347\u2192  margin: 0;\n   348\u2192}\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192.welcome-description {\n   351\u2192  font-size: 1rem;\n   352\u2192  line-height: 1.6;\n   353\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-600);\n   354\u2192  margin: 0;\n   355\u2192}\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192.welcome-cta {\n   358\u2192  margin-top: 0.5rem;\n   359\u2192}\n   360\u2192</style>\n   361\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. USERMESSAGE #14
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
Perfect! Here's a comprehensive summary of the files you requested:

## 1. AppHeader.vue
**Location:** `/home/gem/project/components/AppHeader.vue`

This is a header component that displays:
- App title "OhMyDoc" in the center
- A **Document Type Dropdown** (left side of controls) showing document type options filtered from the active document type registry
- A **Template Switcher Dropdown** (middle) showing templates filtered by the currently selected document type
- A right-actions slot for buttons like "Export PDF"
- Uses `@nuxt/ui` components (`UContainer`, `USelectMenu`)
- Integrates with `useTemplate()` and `useDocumentType()` composables to manage reactive state

## 2. useTemplate.ts
**Location:** `/home/gem/project/composables/useTemplate.ts`

This composable manages the template system:
- Provides reactive template selection shared across components via `useState`
- Persists template choice to `localStorage` (`ohmydoc_template` key)
- Imports **7 templates**:
  - CoverLetterModern
  - CoverLetterClassic
  - CoverLetterMinimal
  - CoverLetterExecutive (new)
  - CoverLetterCreative (new)
  - ResignationProfessional (new)
  - ResignationBrief (new)
- Each template has metadata with `name`, `displayName`, `description`, and `documentTypes` array
- Exposes functions:
  - `setActiveTemplate(name)` - switches template and persists
  - `currentTemplate` - computed reactive component
  - `currentTemplateMetadata` - computed metadata
  - `getTemplatesForDocumentType(docType)` - filters templates by document type

## 3. index.vue (Main Page)
**Location:** `/home/gem/project/pages/index.vue`

The main application page implementing MVP 6:
- **Welcome Screen**: Shown on first load when no saved data exists, with "Start with a sample" CTA
- **Dual-Panel Layout** (50/50 CSS Grid split):
  - Left: XmlEditor component (dark Monaco editor background)
  - Right: PreviewPanel component (light gray background)
- **Features**:
  - 300ms debounced XML parsing for performance
  - Persists XML content to localStorage (`ohmydoc_xml_content`)
  - "Export PDF" button triggers `window.print()`
  - Document type switching loads the appropriate sample XML
  - Responsive design (stacks vertically below 1024px)
  - Print-optimized styles hide all UI chrome, showing only the preview document
- Uses `useDocumentType()` composable to get active document type and sample paths

## Templates Directory Structure
All templates are located under `/home/gem/project/templates/`:

```
templates/
├── classic/
│   ├── CoverLetterClassic.vue
│   └── styles.css
├── creative/
│   ├── CoverLetterCreative.vue
│   └── styles.css
├── executive/
│   ├── CoverLetterExecutive.vue
│   └── styles.css
├── minimal/
│   ├── CoverLetterMinimal.vue
│   └── styles.css
├── modern/
│   ├── CoverLetterModern.vue
│   └── styles.css
├── resignation-brief/
│   ├── ResignationBrief.vue
│   └── styles.css
└── resignation-professional/
    ├── ResignationProfessional.vue
    └── styles.css
```

Each template directory contains:
- A Vue Single File Component (the template itself)
- A `styles.css` file with scoped/template-specific styling

    24→            value-key="value"
    25→            label-key="label"
    26→            size="xs"
    27→            class="w-36"
    28→          />
    29→        </div>
    30→
    31→        <!-- Template Switcher Dropdown (filtered by active document type) -->
    32→        <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
    33→          <label class="text-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-gray-500 whitespace-nowrap">Template</label>
    34→          <USelectMenu
    35→            v-model="selectedTemplate"
    36→            :items="templateOptions"
    37→            value-key="value"
    38→            label-key="label"
    39→            size="xs"
    40→            class="w-28"
    41→          />
    42→        </div>
    43→
    44→        <slot name="right-actions" />
    45→      </div>
    46→    </div>
    47→  </UContainer>
    48→</template>
    49→
    50→<style scoped>
    51→.app-header {
    52→  padding-top: 0.5rem;
    53→  padding-bottom: 0.5rem;
    54→}
    55→</style>
    56→
    57→<script setup lang="ts">
    58→import { computed } from 'vue'
    59→import { useTemplate } from '~/composables/useTemplate'
    60→import { useDocumentType } from '~/composables/useDocumentType'
    61→
    62→const { activeTemplate, setActiveTemplate, getTemplatesForDocumentType } = useTemplate()
    63→const { activeDocumentType, setActiveDocumentType, getAvailableDocumentTypes } = useDocumentType()
    64→
    65→// Document type options (static — registry doesn't change at runtime)
    66→const documentTypeOptions = getAvailableDocumentTypes().map(d => ({ label: d.displayName, value: d.name }))
    67→
    68→// Template options re-computed whenever the active document type changes
    69→const templateOptions = computed(() =>
    70→  getTemplatesForDocumentType(activeDocumentType.value).map(m => ({ label: m.displayName, value: m.name })),
    71→)
    72→
    73→const selectedDocumentType = computed({
    74→  get: () => activeDocumentType.value,
    75→  set: (val: string) => setActiveDocumentType(val),
    76→})
    77→
    78→const selectedTemplate = computed({
    79→  get: () => activeTemplate.value,
    80→  set: (val: string) => setActiveTemplate(val),
    81→})
    82→</script>
    83→

USERMESSAGE #19
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Template System Composable
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides reactive template selection and management for document rendering.
     5→ * Active template is shared across all components via useState and persisted
     6→ * to localStorage so the selection survives page reloads.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import { onMounted, computed } from 'vue'
    10→import type { Component } from 'vue'
    11→import CoverLetterModern from '~/templates/modern/CoverLetterModern.vue'
    12→import CoverLetterClassic from '~/templates/classic/CoverLetterClassic.vue'
    13→import CoverLetterMinimal from '~/templates/minimal/CoverLetterMinimal.vue'
    14→import CoverLetterExecutive from '~/templates/executive/CoverLetterExecutive.vue'
    15→import CoverLetterCreative from '~/templates/creative/CoverLetterCreative.vue'
    16→import ResignationProfessional from '~/templates/resignation-professional/ResignationProfessional.vue'
    17→import ResignationBrief from '~/templates/resignation-brief/ResignationBrief.vue'
    18→
    19→const LS_KEY = 'ohmydoc_template'
    20→
    21→// Template metadata interface
    22→export interface TemplateMetadata {
    23→  name: string
    24→  displayName: string
    25→  description: string
    26→  /** Document type names this template supports (matches DocumentTypeConfig.name) */
    27→  documentTypes: string[]
    28→}
    29→
    30→// Template registry type
    31→export interface TemplateRegistry {
    32→  [key: string]: {
    33→    component: Component
    34→    metadata: TemplateMetadata
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Template Registry
    40→ *
    41→ * Maps template names to their Vue components and metadata.
    42→ * All templates use the same ParsedData props interface for consistency.
    43→ */
    44→const templates: TemplateRegistry = {
    45→  modern: {
    46→    component: CoverLetterModern,
    47→    metadata: {
    48→      name: 'modern',
    49→      displayName: 'Modern',
    50→      description: 'Professional cover letter template with modern styling and clean typography',
    51→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    52→    },
    53→  },
    54→  classic: {
    55→    component: CoverLetterClassic,
    56→    metadata: {
    57→      name: 'classic',
    58→      displayName: 'Classic',
    59→      description: 'Traditional cover letter with table-based layout and formal styling',
    60→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    61→    },
    62→  },
    63→  minimal: {
    64→    component: CoverLetterMinimal,
    65→    metadata: {
    66→      name: 'minimal',
    67→      displayName: 'Minimal',
    68→      description: 'Clean, minimalist cover letter design with simple structure and generous whitespace',
    69→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    70→    },
    71→  },
    72→  executive: {
    73→    component: CoverLetterExecutive,
    74→    metadata: {
    75→      name: 'executive',
    76→      displayName: 'Executive',
    77→      description: 'Bold, confident serif template with navy accent, large name treatment, and horizontal rule dividers',
    78→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    79→    },
    80→  },
    81→  creative: {
    82→    component: CoverLetterCreative,
    83→    metadata: {
    84→      name: 'creative',
    85→      displayName: 'Creative',
    86→      description: 'Two-column layout with vibrant accent sidebar — designed for designers, creatives, and marketers',
    87→      documentTypes: ['cover-letter'],
    88→    },
    89→  },
    90→  'resignation-professional': {
    91→    component: ResignationProfessional,
    92→    metadata: {
    93→      name: 'resignation-professional',
    94→      displayName: 'Professional',
    95→      description: 'Formal letterhead layout with sender top-left, date top-right, and clean typography',
    96→      documentTypes: ['resignation-letter'],
    97→    },
    98→  },
    99→  'resignation-brief': {
   100→    component: ResignationBrief,
   101→    metadata: {
   102→      name: 'resignation-brief',
   103→      displayName: 'Brief',
   104→      description: 'Compact, minimal resignation letter with just the essentials',
   105→      documentTypes: ['resignation-letter'],
   106→    },
   107→  },
   108→}
   109→
   110→/**
   111→ * Composable function to use the reactive template system in Vue components.
   112→ *
   113→ * Active template state is shared across all component instances via useState.
   114→ * On mount, the saved localStorage value is restored (client-side only).
   115→ *
   116→ * @returns Reactive template state and control functions
   117→ */
   118→export function useTemplate() {
   119→  // Shared reactive state — useState ensures all components see the same value
   120→  const activeTemplate = useState<string>('activeTemplate', () => 'modern')
   121→
   122→  // Restore from localStorage after hydration (client-only, runs once per component mount)
   123→  onMounted(() => {
   124→    try {
   125→      const saved = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY)
   126→      if (saved && templates[saved]) {
   127→        activeTemplate.value = saved
   128→      }
   129→    }
   130→    catch {
   131→      // Ignore storage errors (private browsing, quota exceeded, etc.)
   132→    }
   133→  })
   134→
   135→  /**
   136→   * Switch to a different template and persist the choice to localStorage.
   137→   */
   138→  function setActiveTemplate(name: string) {
   139→    if (!templates[name]) {
   140→      console.warn(`Template "${name}" not found. Available: ${Object.keys(templates).join(', ')}`)
   141→      return
   142→    }
   143→    activeTemplate.value = name
   144→    try {
   145→      localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, name)
   146→    }
   147→    catch {
   148→      // Ignore storage errors
   149→    }
   150→  }
   151→
   152→  /** Reactive component for the currently active template */
   153→  const currentTemplate = computed<Component | undefined>(
   154→    () => templates[activeTemplate.value]?.component,
   155→  )
   156→
   157→  /** Metadata for the currently active template */
   158→  const currentTemplateMetadata = computed<TemplateMetadata | undefined>(
   159→    () => templates[activeTemplate.value]?.metadata,
   160→  )
   161→
   162→  /** Names of all registered templates */
   163→  function getAvailableTemplates(): string[] {
   164→    return Object.keys(templates)
   165→  }
   166→
   167→  /** Metadata objects for all registered templates */
   168→  function getAllTemplateMetadata(): TemplateMetadata[] {
   169→    return Object.values(templates).map(t => t.metadata)
   170→  }
   171→
   172→  /** Metadata objects for templates that support the given document type */
   173→  function getTemplatesForDocumentType(docType: string): TemplateMetadata[] {
   174→    return Object.values(templates)
   175→      .filter(t => t.metadata.documentTypes.includes(docType))
   176→      .map(t => t.metadata)
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    activeTemplate,
   181→    setActiveTemplate,
   182→    currentTemplate,
   183→    currentTemplateMetadata,
   184→    getAvailableTemplates,
   185→    getAllTemplateMetadata,
   186→    getTemplatesForDocumentType,
   187→  }
   188→}
   189→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #20
Now I have a good understanding of the current implementation. Let me plan the changes needed: 1. Create a new `TemplateSelector.vue` component that shows visual preview cards 2. Modify `AppHeader.vue` to use the new component instead of `USelectMenu` 3. Create CSS-only "layout sketches" for each template Let me also check if there are any existing CSS patterns I should follow and look at the template styles for inspiration. The plan: 1. Create a `TemplateSelector.vue` component with: - A "Change Template" button in AppHeader that opens a popover/panel - Visual preview cards showing CSS layout sketches - Active/selected state highlight - Only shows templates for the active document type Each template will have its own distinct visual style: Modern with a clean single column and blue accent, Classic with centered content and serif styling, Minimal with lots of whitespace, Executive with a bold header block in navy, Creative with a sidebar layout, and the resignation templates with specific positioning for sender info and dates. I'll implement these as CSS-only sketches. Now I'm building the TemplateSelector component as a popover triggered from the header, with cards that visually represent each template's layout through geometric shapes and lines rather than actual content. Building the template selector component... Writing template sketches... Building the template selector component... Actually, I need to verify the UPopover API for the version they're using—the props might differ in @nuxt/ui v4, so let me check the existing code to see what's already implemented. Looking at the commit history, they're definitely on @nuxt/ui v4, which means UPopover uses a `#content` slot for the popover body and the default slot as the trigger. Now I'm planning out the CSS sketches for each template layout—I'll use CSS custom properties and pseudo-elements to create visual representations of the different designs, starting with a modern style that has a clean blue accent bar. Writing the sketch styles... Still writing component styles... Building the template selector component... component that: 1. Shows a button with the current template name 2. Opens a popover with template cards 3. Each card has a CSS-only sketch and the template name For the sketches, I'll use a combination of `background`, `border`, and pseudo-elements to create simple layout representations. The sketches will be pure CSS in the `<style scoped>` block. Let me now write the actual implementation. I'll need to: 1. Create `components/TemplateSelector.vue` 2. Modify `components/AppHeader.vue` to use it For the UPopover in @nuxt/ui v4, let me check how it's actually used. Looking at the nuxt/ui v4 docs/source, UPopover has a default slot (trigger) and `#content` slot (panel content). Let me write the component now. I'll create clean CSS sketches for each template... Writing template card styles... Building the template cards... I'm realizing the template structure is getting unwieldy, so I'll simplify by using CSS background patterns with gradients instead of nested divs to keep the markup clean. I'll create a 72x88px canvas for each sketch template using absolutely positioned divs within a relative container, and stick with v-if blocks since there are only seven templates to handle. For the line patterns, I can use repeating linear gradients to simulate text lines without extra HTML elements. Now I'm mapping out each template design—Modern gets an accent bar with header and lined body, Classic uses a centered header with divider and lined content, Minimal keeps it sparse with just a thin top and minimal lines, and Executive... Still designing templates... Writing the template selector component... Building the template selector grid... Still writing styles... Still writing styles... Still writing styles... Still writing styles... Still writing sketch styles... Still writing styles... Still writing styles... Still writing styles... Still writing styles... I'm realizing the CSS pseudo-element approach has limitations since I can only create two extra elements per div, but I need to render multiple lines and specific child elements like names and titles. I think the better path is to create separate component structures for each template type—using Vue's conditional rendering to swap between different HTML layouts rather than trying to force everything into a single CSS-based solution. Building the template structure... I'm realizing that using conditional rendering for each sketch type inside the loop would get messy, so I should switch to a cleaner approach where I map each template variant to its corresponding structure and styling rules. The simplest solution is to always render a single inner div per sketch container and conditionally position or hide it depending on the template type—that way I avoid complex conditional logic while keeping the markup consistent. For the implementation, I'll create the TemplateSelector component with a trigger button displaying the current template, wrap it in a popover with a grid of template cards, and each card will show a sketch preview with its label. Then I'll swap out the template menu in AppHeader with this new component and verify dark mode styling works properly. Building the component now... Writing the TemplateSelector component...
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

USERMESSAGE #23
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Document Type Registry Composable
     3→ *
     4→ * Manages the registry of supported document types and the currently active
     5→ * document type. Each document type bundles its own parser, validator,
     6→ * available templates, and sample XML path.
     7→ *
     8→ * Architecture note: this composable is the single source of truth for
     9→ * "what document type am I editing right now?" All other composables and
    10→ * components that need type-specific behaviour should consult it.
    11→ */
    12→
    13→import { computed, onMounted } from 'vue'
    14→import { parseXml as parseCoverLetterXml, validateXml as validateCoverLetterXml } from '~/composables/useXmlParser'
    15→import type { ValidationResult, ParseResult } from '~/composables/useXmlParser'
    16→import { parseResignationXml, validateResignationXml } from '~/composables/useResignationParser'
    17→
    18→const LS_KEY = 'ohmydoc_document_type'
    19→
    20→// ─── Public interfaces ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21→
    22→export interface DocumentTypeConfig {
    23→  /** Internal key used in URLs, localStorage, and template associations */
    24→  name: string
    25→  /** Human-readable label shown in the UI */
    26→  displayName: string
    27→  /** Short description of the document type */
    28→  description: string
    29→  /** Path to the bundled sample XML (relative to /public) */
    30→  sampleXmlPath: string
    31→  /** Template to activate when this document type is first selected */
    32→  defaultTemplate: string
    33→  /** Names of templates registered in useTemplate that support this type */
    34→  templates: string[]
    35→  /** Parse an XML string into a typed data object for template rendering */
    36→  parse: (xmlString: string) => ParseResult
    37→  /** Validate well-formedness and schema requirements without full parsing */
    38→  validate: (xmlString: string) => ValidationResult
    39→}
    40→
    41→// ─── Registry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    42→
    43→/**
    44→ * All registered document types.
    45→ *
    46→ * To add a new type, append an entry here following the same shape as
    47→ * 'cover-letter'. The parser and validator functions live in their own
    48→ * composable (e.g. useResumParser.ts) and are imported at the top of this file.
    49→ */
    50→const documentTypes: Record<string, DocumentTypeConfig> = {
    51→  'cover-letter': {
    52→    name: 'cover-letter',
    53→    displayName: 'Cover Letter',
    54→    description: 'Professional cover letter for job applications',
    55→    sampleXmlPath: '/samples/cover-letter.xml',
    56→    defaultTemplate: 'modern',
    57→    templates: ['modern', 'classic', 'minimal'],
    58→    parse: parseCoverLetterXml,
    59→    validate: validateCoverLetterXml,
    60→  },
    61→  'resignation-letter': {
    62→    name: 'resignation-letter',
    63→    displayName: 'Resignation Letter',
    64→    description: 'Formal resignation letter to notify an employer',
    65→    sampleXmlPath: '/samples/resignation-letter.xml',
    66→    defaultTemplate: 'resignation-professional',
    67→    templates: ['resignation-professional', 'resignation-brief'],
    68→    parse: parseResignationXml,
    69→    validate: validateResignationXml,
    70→  },
    71→}
    72→
    73→// ─── Composable ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    74→
    75→/**
    76→ * Reactive document type selection.
    77→ *
    78→ * State is shared across all component instances via useState and persisted
    79→ * to localStorage so the selection survives page reloads.
    80→ */
    81→export function useDocumentType() {
    82→  const activeDocumentType = useState<string>('activeDocumentType', () => 'cover-letter')
    83→
    84→  // Restore from localStorage after hydration (client-only)
    85→  onMounted(() => {
    86→    try {
    87→      const saved = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY)
    88→      if (saved && documentTypes[saved]) {
    89→        activeDocumentType.value = saved
    90→      }
    91→    }
    92→    catch {
    93→      // Ignore storage errors (private browsing, quota exceeded, etc.)
    94→    }
    95→  })
    96→
    97→  /**
    98→   * Switch to a different document type and persist the choice to localStorage.
    99→   * Also resets the active template to that type's default.
   100→   */
   101→  function setActiveDocumentType(name: string) {
   102→    if (!documentTypes[name]) {
   103→      console.warn(`Document type "${name}" not found. Available: ${Object.keys(documentTypes).join(', ')}`)
   104→      return
   105→    }
   106→    activeDocumentType.value = name
   107→    try {
   108→      localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, name)
   109→    }
   110→    catch {
   111→      // Ignore storage errors
   112→    }
   113→  }
   114→
   115→  /** Config object for the currently active document type */
   116→  const currentDocumentType = computed<DocumentTypeConfig | undefined>(
   117→    () => documentTypes[activeDocumentType.value],
   118→  )
   119→
   120→  /** All registered document type config objects */
   121→  function getAvailableDocumentTypes(): DocumentTypeConfig[] {
   122→    return Object.values(documentTypes)
   123→  }
   124→
   125→  return {
   126→    activeDocumentType,
   127→    currentDocumentType,
   128→    setActiveDocumentType,
   129→    getAvailableDocumentTypes,
   130→  }
   131→}
   132→

    "content": "     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192 * Document Type Registry Composable\n     3\u2192 *\n     4\u2192 * Manages the registry of supported document types and the currently active\n     5\u2192 * document type. Each document type bundles its own parser, validator,\n     6\u2192 * available templates, and sample XML path.\n     7\u2192 *\n     8\u2192 * Architecture note: this composable is the single source of truth for\n     9\u2192 * \"what document type am I editing right now?\" All other composables and\n    10\u2192 * components that need type-specific behaviour should consult it.\n    11\u2192 */\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192import { computed, onMounted } from 'vue'\n    14\u2192import { parseXml as parseCoverLetterXml, validateXml as validateCoverLetterXml } from '~/composables/useXmlParser'\n the bundled sample XML (relative to /public) */\n    30\u2192  sampleXmlPath: string\n    31\u2192  /** Template to activate when this document type is first selected */\n    32\u2192 USERMESSAGE #25
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Creative Template Styles
     3→ *
     4→ * Bold, visually distinctive cover letter for designers, creatives, and marketers.
     5→ * Two-column layout: vibrant accent sidebar (left) + clean white content area (right).
     6→ * Sans-serif throughout for a modern, punchy feel.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→:root {
    10→  --cr-font: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
    11→  --cr-max-width: 780px;
    12→  --cr-sidebar-width: 210px;
    13→  --cr-sidebar-bg: #5e60ce;
    14→  --cr-sidebar-text: #fff;
    15→  --cr-sidebar-muted: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.72);
    16→  --cr-accent: #48bfe3;
    17→  --cr-ink: #1a1a2e;
    18→  --cr-muted: #555570;
    19→  --cr-line: 1.65;
    20→}
    21→
    22→* {
    23→  box-sizing: border-box;
    24→}
    25→
    26→/* ── Two-column layout ───────────────────────────────────── */
    27→
    28→.application-document {
    29→  display: grid;
    30→  grid-template-columns: var(--cr-sidebar-width) 1fr;
    31→  max-width: var(--cr-max-width);
    32→  min-height: 900px;
    33→  margin: 0 auto;
    34→  background: #fff;
    35→  font-family: var(--cr-font);
    36→  color: var(--cr-ink);
    37→  font-size: 15px;
    38→  line-height: var(--cr-line);
    39→}
    40→
    41→/* ── Sidebar ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    42→
    43→.sidebar {
    44→  background: var(--cr-sidebar-bg);
    45→  color: var(--cr-sidebar-text);
    46→  padding: 2.5rem 1.25rem 2rem;
    47→  display: flex;
    48→  flex-direction: column;
    49→  gap: 0;
    50→  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
    51→  print-color-adjust: exact;
    52→}
    53→
    54→.applicant-name {
    55→  font-size: 1.45rem;
    56→  font-weight: 800;
    57→  line-height: 1.2;
    58→  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
    59→  color: #fff;
    60→  margin: 0 0 1.25rem 0;
    61→  word-break: break-word;
    62→}
    63→
    64→.sidebar-divider {
    65→  width: 2.5rem;
    66→  height: 3px;
    67→  background: var(--cr-accent);
    68→  border-radius: 2px;
    69→  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
    70→}
    71→
    72→.applicant-address {
    73→  font-style: normal;
    74→  font-size: 0.82rem;
    75→  color: var(--cr-sidebar-muted);
    76→  line-height: 1.6;
    77→  margin: 0 0 1.25rem 0;
    78→}
    79→
    80→.contact-information {
    81→  display: flex;
    82→  flex-direction: column;
    83→  gap: 0.45rem;
    84→  margin-top: auto;
    85→  padding-top: 1rem;
    86→}
    87→
    88→.contact-item {
    89→  font-size: 0.78rem;
    90→  color: var(--cr-sidebar-muted);
    91→  word-break: break-all;
    92→  text-decoration: none;
    93→}
    94→
    95→.contact-item.email {
    96→  color: var(--cr-accent);
    97→  text-underline-offset: 2px;
    98→}
    99→
   100→.contact-item.email:hover {
   101→  text-decoration: underline;
   102→}
   103→
   104→/* ── Main content area ───────────────────────────────────── */
   105→
   106→.main-content {
   107→  padding: 2.5rem 2rem 2rem;
   108→  display: flex;
   109→  flex-direction: column;
   110→  gap: 1.25rem;
   111→}
   112→
   113→/* ── Date ────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   114→
   115→.document-date {
   116→  font-size: 0.85rem;
   117→  color: var(--cr-muted);
   118→  text-align: right;
   119→}
   120→
   121→/* ── Recipient ───────────────────────────────────────────── */
   122→
   123→.recipient {
   124→  border-left: 3px solid var(--cr-sidebar-bg);
   125→  padding-left: 0.85rem;
   126→}
   127→
   128→.recipient-position {
   129→  margin: 0 0 0.1rem 0;
   130→  font-weight: 700;
   131→  font-size: 0.95rem;
   132→  color: var(--cr-ink);
   133→}
   134→
   135→.recipient-company {
   136→  margin: 0 0 0.15rem 0;
   137→  font-size: 0.9rem;
   138→  color: var(--cr-ink);
   139→}
   140→
   141→.recipient-address {
   142→  font-style: normal;
   143→  font-size: 0.82rem;
   144→  color: var(--cr-muted);
   145→  line-height: 1.5;
   146→  margin: 0;
   147→}
   148→
   149→/* ── Letter body ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
   150→
   151→.letter {
   152→  display: flex;
   153→  flex-direction: column;
   154→  gap: 0.9rem;
   155→}
   156→
   157→.letter > p {
   158→  margin: 0;
   159→  line-height: var(--cr-line);
   160→}
   161→
   162→.salutation {
   163→  font-weight: 600;
   164→}
   165→
   166→.experience-section {
   167→  display: flex;
   168→  flex-direction: column;
   169→  gap: 0.75rem;
   170→}
   171→
   172→.experience-employer {
   173→  margin: 0 0 0.25rem 0;
   174→  font-weight: 700;
   175→  color: var(--cr-sidebar-bg);
   176→  font-size: 0.95rem;
   177→}
   178→
   179→.achievements {
   180→  margin: 0 0 0 1.25rem;
   181→  padding: 0;
   182→  list-style-type: none;
   183→}
   184→
   185→.achievement {
   186→  position: relative;
   187→  padding-left: 1rem;
   188→  margin: 0.2rem 0;
   189→  line-height: var(--cr-line);
   190→  color: var(--cr-ink);
   191→}
   192→
   193→.achievement::before {
   194→  content: '';
   195→  position: absolute;
   196→  left: 0;
   197→  top: 0.6em;
   198→  width: 5px;
   199→  height: 5px;
   200→  background: var(--cr-accent);
   201→  border-radius: 50%;
   202→}
   203→
   204→.signature {
   205→  font-weight: 700;
   206→  color: var(--cr-sidebar-bg);
   207→  margin-top: 1.5rem;
   208→}
   209→
   210→/* ── Print ───────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   211→
   212→@media print {
   213→  .application-document {
   214→    max-width: none;
   215→    /* @page in app.vue provides 2cm physical margins; no extra CSS margin needed */
   216→    margin: 0;
   217→    /* Ensure two-column grid is preserved in print */
   218→    display: grid;
   219→    grid-template-columns: var(--cr-sidebar-width) 1fr;
   220→    /* Remove screen min-height; let content determine height */
   221→    min-height: unset;
   222→  }
   223→
   224→  /* Force sidebar background colour to print (browsers suppress backgrounds by default) */
   225→  .sidebar {
   226→    -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
   227→    print-color-adjust: exact;
   228→  }
   229→
   230→  /* Prevent sidebar from breaking across pages */
   231→  .sidebar,
   232→  .main-content {
   233→    page-break-inside: avoid;
   234→    break-inside: avoid;
   235→  }
   236→
   237→  /* Avoid splitting experience blocks */
   238→  .experience-section > * {
   239→    page-break-inside: avoid;
   240→    break-inside: avoid;
   241→  }
   242→
   243→  .experience-employer {
   244→    page-break-after: avoid;
   245→    break-after: avoid;
   246→  }
   247→
   248→  /* Prevent orphaned/widowed lines */
   249→  .letter > p {
   250→    orphans: 3;
   251→    widows: 3;
   252→  }
   253→
   254→  .contact-item.email {
   255→    text-decoration: none;
   256→  }
   257→}
   258→
   259→/* ── Responsive: stack on mobile ────────────────────────── */
   260→
   261→@media screen and (max-width: 640px) {
   262→  .application-document {
   263→    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
   264→    min-height: unset;
   265→  }
   266→
   267→  .sidebar {
   268→    padding: 1.5rem 1.25rem;
   269→  }
   270→
   271→  .applicant-name {
   272→    font-size: 1.3rem;
   273→  }
   274→
   275→  .contact-information {
   276→    flex-direction: row;
   277→    flex-wrap: wrap;
   278→    gap: 0.5rem;
   279→    padding-top: 0.75rem;
   280→    margin-top: 0.75rem;
   281→  }
   282→
   283→  .main-content {
   284→    padding: 1.5rem 1.25rem;
   285→  }
   286→}
   287→
